heading: Maintenance and administration
label: 9301 Maintenance and administration
text: The Director of the National Park Service shall maintain and administer Theodore Roosevelt Island as a natural park for the recreation and enjoyment of the public.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)930140:124 (words before proviso).(words before proviso), ; , .May 21, 1932, ch. 200, § 147 Stat. 163Feb. 11, 1933, ch. 48, § 147 Stat. 799In this chapter, the words “Director of the National Park Service” are substituted for “Director of Public Buildings and Public Parks of the National Capital” and “director” because of section 2 of Executive Order No. 6166 (eff. ) and the Act of (ch. 38, ).June 10, 1933June 10, 193448 Stat. 389In this section, the text of section 1 (words before 1st semicolon) of the Act of (ch. 200, ) is omitted as executed.May 21, 193247 Stat. 163

Designation of Theodore Roosevelt Island, , provided that:Act Feb. 11, 1933, ch. 48, § 247 Stat. 799“In all public documents, records, and maps of the United States in which Roosevelt Island is designated or referred to it shall be designated as ‘Theodore Roosevelt Island’.”
